articles.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
articles.summary: Elon Musk and Sam Altman face off in court over OpenAI's mission. Musk accuses OpenAI of abandoning its nonprofit goals to prioritize profits, with opening arguments beginning April 27th.

articles.title: Claude can now plug directly into Photoshop, Blender, and Ableton
articles.summary: Anthropic launched Claude connectors for creative software including Photoshop, Blender, and Ableton, allowing the AI chatbot to integrate directly with popular design and music tools.

articles.title: Musk and Altman go to court
articles.summary: Elon Musk and OpenAI's Sam Altman are in court battling over early AI development credit and compensation. The trial is expected to expose industry secrets as both sides fight over who deserves recognition and funds.

articles.title: Lovable launches its vibe-coding app on iOS and Android
articles.summary: Lovable launches a vibe-coding app on iOS and Android, enabling developers to code web apps and websites remotely using an intuitive interface.

articles.title: YouTube is testing an AI-powered search feature that shows guided answers
articles.summary: YouTube tests AI-powered search with guided answers for Premium subscribers in the U.S. The feature is available on an opt-in basis.

articles.title: BCI startup Neurable looks to license its ‘mind-reading’ tech for consumer wearables
articles.summary: Neurable, a BCI startup, aims to license non-invasive mind-reading technology for consumer wearables. The neural data collection tech could enable various consumer applications.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The platform has grown to 2 million developers by addressing limitations of legacy cloud infrastructure for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative running locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges. The unconventional $5,000 marketing stunt generated thousands of applicants and helped the startup attract top engineering talent.

articles.title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials
articles.summary: A popular open-source package with 1 million monthly downloads was compromised, allowing attackers to steal user credentials including API tokens, SSH keys, and cloud provider credentials through a malicious version.

articles.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
articles.summary: Top university websites including Berkeley, Columbia, and Washington University are serving pornography and malware through neglected subdomains due to poor administrative record-keeping. Hundreds of subdomains across 34 universities have been exploited by scammers.

articles.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
articles.summary: Kyber ransomware claims to use quantum-safe ML-KEM encryption, making it the first ransomware family to incorporate NIST-standardized post-quantum cryptography for protecting encrypted files.

articles.title: The Download: Musk and Altman’s legal showdown, and AI’s profit problem
articles.summary: Elon Musk and Sam Altman head to court over OpenAI's future, with Musk seeking $134 billion in damages and demanding the company return to non-profit status. The trial could determine whether OpenAI can proceed as a for-profit enterprise ahead of its IPO.

articles.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
articles.summary: Elon Musk sues OpenAI's Sam Altman, claiming he was deceived about the company's nonprofit status. The trial could force OpenAI to remain nonprofit and potentially remove current leadership before its IPO.

articles.title: The missing step between hype and profit
articles.summary: AI activists highlight the gap between AI hype and practical implementation, questioning what happens between developing advanced AI and achieving profitable outcomes. The movement calls for pausing AI development until clearer societal benefits are established.

articles.title: Introducing NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni: Long-Context Multimodal Intelligence for Documents, Audio and Video Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA releases Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a multimodal AI model supporting long-context processing for documents, audio, and video. Enables intelligent agents for diverse media types.

articles.title: How to build scalable web apps with OpenAI's Privacy Filter
articles.summary: Learn to build scalable web applications using OpenAI's Privacy Filter to protect user data while maintaining performance and security.

articles.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
articles.summary: DeepSeek-V4 achieves a million-token context window that agents can effectively utilize, significantly expanding AI's ability to process and understand vast amounts of information in single interactions.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Microsoft's Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative capabilities. Companies use AI for content handling and imaginative marketing solutions.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, providing developers with resources to build AI-powered agricultural solutions for sustainable farming practices.

articles.title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
articles.summary: Google Translate celebrates 20 years of service, evolving from a 2006 AI experiment to supporting nearly 250 languages today. The company shares fun facts and new features marking this milestone.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.summary: Google and Kaggle are offering a 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with registration now open. This Vibe Coding Course provides hands-on training in AI agent development.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Gemini offers AI-powered tips for organizing both physical homes and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox decluttering, and seasonal chore management.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new distributed AI training method that improves resilience and efficiency. The approach enables more reliable training across multiple systems with reduced communication overhead.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations worldwide implement frontier AI technology and accelerate their digital transformation efforts.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token prices, claiming improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia released DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that enhances video game graphics in real-time with photorealistic improvements. The technology has applications beyond gaming.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in complex indoor environments by improving target localization, self-localization, and path planning capabilities.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are leaking sensitive API keys into public code repositories and package registries, posing significant security risks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, representing a major supply chain security risk.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but reduces their ability to explore solutions on complex problems, revealing a fundamental tradeoff between efficiency and generalization.

articles.title: Top 10 Physical AI Models Powering Real-World Robots in 2026
articles.summary: The article lists the top 10 physical AI models driving real-world robotics in 2026, featuring systems from NVIDIA, Google DeepMind, Physical Intelligence, and other leading companies.

articles.title: How to Build a Lightweight Vision-Language-Action-Inspired Embodied Agent with Latent World Modeling and Model Predictive Control
articles.summary: Tutorial on building a lightweight embodied agent using vision-language-action principles. The agent learns from pixel observations through a world model and model predictive control in latent space.

articles.title: Meet Talkie-1930: A 13B Open-Weight LLM Trained on Pre-1931 English Text for Historical Reasoning and Generalization Research
articles.summary: Talkie-1930 is a 13-billion parameter open-weight LLM trained exclusively on pre-1931 English text, creating a 'vintage language model' with a historical knowledge cutoff for studying historical reasoning and generalization.

articles.title: User interfaces as we know them are dead - 4 ways to prep for 'disposable' UIs
articles.summary: User interfaces are evolving from static screens to dynamically generated, just-in-time projection layers appearing as simple text boxes, making traditional UIs obsolete.

articles.title: Want a free Apple Watch? T-Mobile will give you the SE 3 - how to get yours today
articles.summary: T-Mobile is offering a free Apple Watch SE 3 with a new smartwatch line on your T-Mobile plan. This deal applies to Apple's latest entry-level smartwatch model.

articles.title: You can save 50% on this Sony soundbar right now - but the deal ends tonight
articles.summary: Sony's 5.1CH soundbar is on sale at Best Buy with a $500 discount, ending tonight. Enhance your TV's audio quality at a significant savings.

articles.title: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Developer’s Guide
articles.summary: Guide on context engineering for AI agents, helping developers optimize how information is provided to AI systems for better performance and accuracy.

articles.title: Text Summarization with Scikit-LLM
articles.summary: Scikit-LLM enables text summarization using machine learning techniques. The tool integrates with scikit-learn for efficient natural language processing tasks.

articles.title: Building AI Agents with Local Small Language Models
articles.summary: Building AI agents with local small language models is now accessible beyond large tech companies, democratizing AI development. This enables organizations to create custom agents using smaller, more manageable models.

articles.title: Let the AI Do the Experimenting
articles.summary: Autoresearch uses AI to optimize marketing campaigns while operating within budget constraints. This automated approach enables efficient experimentation without manual intervention.

articles.title: Correlation Doesn’t Mean Causation! But What Does It Mean?
articles.summary: The article explores what correlation actually means in data analysis, clarifying the common misconception that correlation implies causation. It examines the practical implications of understanding correlation without assuming causal relationships.

articles.title: The Next Frontier of AI in Production Is Chaos Engineering
articles.summary: AI production systems need chaos engineering to understand failure impacts and learn from controlled disruptions. While blast-radius control has mature tools, intent-based learning requires further development.

articles.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Model, Unifying Vision, Audio and Language for up to 9x More Efficient AI Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA unveiled Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a unified multimodal model integrating vision, audio, and language capabilities into one system, enabling 9x more efficient AI agents with faster processing and advanced reasoning.

articles.title: Into the Omniverse: Manufacturing’s Simulation-First Era Has Arrived
articles.summary: Manufacturing is shifting to simulation-first approaches using NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD, replacing traditional design-build-test cycles with virtual testing environments.

articles.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 powers Codex, an agentic coding application running on NVIDIA's GB200 infrastructure. Over 10,000 NVIDIA employees across departments report significant productivity gains using the new system.

articles.title: Migrating a text agent to a voice assistant with Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
articles.summary: Amazon Nova 2 Sonic enables migrating text agents to voice assistants for real-time, natural interactions. The article guides developers through architecture, design priorities, and best practices for voice assistant implementation across industries.

articles.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni model now available on Amazon SageMaker JumpStart
articles.summary: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a multimodal AI model processing video, audio, image, and text, is now available on Amazon SageMaker JumpStart for enterprise applications.

articles.title: Automate repetitive tasks with Amazon Quick Flows
articles.summary: Amazon Quick Flows automates repetitive tasks using AI workflows without coding. Users describe what to automate in natural language, and the system builds intelligent workflows for data processing and reporting.

articles.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Powers Multimodal Agent Reasoning in a Single Efficient Open Model
articles.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, an efficient open model enabling multimodal agent reasoning across text, audio, video, and documents in a single unified system instead of fragmented model chains.

articles.title: 24/7 Simulation Loops: How Agentic AI Keeps Subsurface Engineering Moving
articles.summary: Agentic AI enables 24/7 continuous simulation loops in subsurface engineering, automating traditionally manual workflows and bridging the gap between data complexity and human capacity.

articles.title: Build with DeepSeek V4 Using NVIDIA Blackwell and GPU-Accelerated Endpoints
articles.summary: DeepSeek launched V4-Pro and V4-Flash models supporting efficient million-token context inference on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. V4-Pro features 1.6T total parameters optimized for high-performance AI applications.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study explores how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, examining generational attitudes toward advertising.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing and sharing technology to reduce its carbon footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: A person with an unconventional background successfully developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advancements in XR.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models that enables practical long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic state exploration, and improved gradient shaping for visual models.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand and interpret how large language models make decisions at scale through feature attribution analysis, advancing AI transparency and safety.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses noisy measurements and noise models to optimize how well imaging systems distinguish objects and capture visual information.
